comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers around appreciation for a visualization of LLM architectures, with users expressing admiration and suggesting enhancements like evolutionary sorting and scaled views. Some users share related resources and inquire about insights gained during the creation process.

comments.author: iroddis
comments.text:
This is amazing, such a nice presentation. It reminds me of the Neural Network Zoo [1], which was also a nice visualization of different architectures.

[1] https://www.asimovinstitute.org/neural-network-zoo/
comments.analysis: The comment provides a relevant comparison to a known visualization, adding context and value to the discussion.

comments.author: wood_spirit
comments.text:
Lovely!

Is there a sort order? Would be so nice to understand the threads of evolutions and revolution in the progression. A bit of a family tree and influence layout? It would also be nice to have a scaled view so you can sense the difference in sizes over time.
comments.analysis: This comment offers constructive feedback and suggestions for improving the visualization's clarity and usefulness.

comments.author: gasi
comments.text: So cool — thanks for sharing! Here’s a zoomable version of the diagram: https://zoomhub.net/LKrpB
comments.analysis: The comment shares a helpful resource that complements the original visualization, enhancing user experience.

comments.author: mvrckhckr
comments.text: What a great idea and nice execution.
comments.analysis: A brief positive reaction that adds little analytical depth but reflects genuine appreciation.

comments.author: FailMore
comments.text: Thanks! This is cool. Can you tell me if you learnt anything interesting/surprising when pulling this together? As in did it teach you something about LLM Architecture that you didn't know before you began?
comments.analysis: The comment poses a thoughtful question but does not provide information or analysis itself.
